name: Pizzettas (r T)
recipeIngredient: 1/2 cup Polenta or Coarsely Ground Cornmeal
1 tablespoon Finely Chopped Rosemary or 1 teaspoon Dried
recipeInstructions:
NOTE: The original recipe used 2 Tbsp olive oil, but I decreased it and added the additional water which was not called for in the recipe.
To Make The Dough:
1. In a glass measuring cup with a lip, combine the water and sugar and stir to mix. Sprinkle the yeast over the top and allow to stand for 10 minutes until it has a frothy head. If no froth forms, discard the yeast and start again.
2. In the bowl of a food processor fitted with the metal blade, combine all-purpose flour, polenta or cornmeal, whole wheat flour, rosemary, olive oil, and salt. Process briefly just to blend.
3. With the motor running, pour the yeast mixture steadily through the feed tube and process until the mixture forms a rough ball on the central stem. If the dough has not formed a ball within 20 seconds, remove the cover and sprinkle with water (1 tablespoon or more), then process again. The dough should be processed for a total time of about 45 seconds.
4. Replace the feed tube cover to prevent air from getting in. Cover the food processor with a towel and let the dough rise for 1-1½ hours, or until doubled, puffed, and softened.
5. Prepare toppings: sautéed onions with poppy seeds and toasted sesame seeds, sliced fresh Italian plum tomatoes, caponata, green bell peppers, and capers (optional).
6. If holding the dough for baking later, form individual pizzas without toppings and place on a refrigerator-safe cookie sheet. Refrigerate up to 2 hours.
7. Preheat oven to 450°F on the middle shelf. Place a baking stone in the oven while preheating if available.
8. Process the dough again briefly, then turn it out onto a lightly floured board. Work the dough into 8-10 equal-sized balls (I made 5 individual pizzas). Place the dough balls on a lightly oiled large baking sheet, covered with a slightly damp towel, and let rest for 15 minutes.
9. Press each ball into a 3-inch round. Add desired toppings and leave a ¼-inch border around the edge.
10. Bake for 10-12 minutes.
